Title: Genomic and structural basis for evolution of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: initial polyamine methylation branch of cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes methylation of spermidine in the first specific step of cocaine biosynthesis and also shows spermidine synthase activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies first committed specialized intermediate for cocaine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables spermidine methylation route leading toward cocaine biosynthesis in Erythroxylaceae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnSPMT1 is the first specific methylation enzyme in cocaine biosynthesis. 2. Comparative genomics suggested the SPMT branch evolved from SPDS through the core-eudicot hexaploidy event. 3. SPMT was retained in rosids but lost in ancestral asterids, helping explain pathway divergence between cocaine and hyoscyamine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; phylogenetic analysis; prior biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome assemblies PRJNA749480 and comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful pathway-entry gene for reconstructing or engineering cocaine-type tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional enzyme distinct from solanaceous PMT route.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: initial polyamine methylation branch of hyoscyamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes methylation of putrescine as the first specific step in hyoscyamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies first committed specialized intermediate for hyoscyamine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables solanaceous route for N-methylputrescine formation and thereby hyoscyamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMT catalyzes the first specific step of hyoscyamine biosynthesis in Solanaceae. 2. The paper concludes PMT likely evolved independently from tandem copies of SPDS in Solanales. 3. Independent PMT recruitment explains divergence from the SPMT-based cocaine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; phylogenetic inference; prior pathway characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: A. acutangulus genome PRJNA752913; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful entry enzyme for engineering medicinal hyoscyamine/scopolamine pathways in Solanaceae or heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not experimentally recharacterized in this paper; included as a core pathway divergence gene emphasized in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: N-methylspermidine conversion step in coca lineage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: removes the aminopropyl group from N-methylspermidine to form N-methylputrescine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies upstream precursor pool for cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports erythroxylaceous N-methylputrescine formation route absent from asterids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOF1 catalyzes conversion of N-methylspermidine to N-methylputrescine in Erythroxylaceae. 2. Phylogenetic analysis suggested the AOF1 branch is present only in rosids. 3. Loss of the ancestral AOF gene in asterids contributed to divergence of tropane alkaloid pathway entry routes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic analysis; prior biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful auxiliary upstream enzyme for reconstructing the coca-specific pathway route
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because Results discuss it as a lineage-divergence determinant, though not newly biochemically validated here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: type III PKS that forms the OGA intermediate required for tropane ring formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for nonenzymatic condensation leading to tropane skeleton
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supplies OGA for hyoscyamine tropane skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaPYKS1 represents a Solanaceae PYKS lineage for OGA production. 2. Comparative genomics indicated PYKS1 emerged in the common ancestor of Solanales. 3. PYKS evolution was independent from EnPKS1/2 in Erythroxylaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important OGA-supplying enzyme for engineering medicinal tropane alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized; evolutionary focus here is origin relative to PYKS2 and EnPKS1/2.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: type III PKS with in vitro catalytic activity similar to AaPYKS1 in OGA formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for tropane skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: provides a potentially redundant OGA-forming enzyme copy in A. acutangulus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaPYKS2 is an ortholog of DsPYKS1 and has lateral root-specific expression. 2. AaPYKS2 showed in vitro catalytic activity similar to AaPYKS1 in OGA formation. 3. Comparative genomics suggested PYKS2 arose from duplication around the shared Solanaceae WGT, leaving A. acutangulus with two potentially redundant PYKS copies.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay with malonyl-CoA; expression analysis; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 15 A. acutangulus transcriptomes SRR15559830-SRR15559844; GenBank MZ819700
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: stacking or dosage optimization of PYKS copies may improve flux into medicinal tropane alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper infers functional redundancy with AaPYKS1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: type III PKS that produces the OGA intermediate required for tropane skeleton formation in cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for nonenzymatic condensation leading to tropane skeleton
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: channels flux into cocaine tropane skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnPKS1 is one of two OGA-producing PKSs in E. novogranatense. 2. Comparative genomics showed EnPKS1/2 are located on duplicated synteny blocks likely created by species-specific WGD. 3. The coca PKS lineage was independently recruited relative to Solanaceae PYKS enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior structural and biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. novogranatense genome PRJNA749480; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important for reconstructing the coca-specific branch of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Independent catalytic innovation relative to PYKS lineage was established in prior work and reinforced here genomically.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: type III PKS that contributes to OGA formation in cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for tropane skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: additional OGA-forming PKS copy that may increase flux into cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnPKS2 is the second E. novogranatense OGA-producing PKS. 2. EnPKS1/2 reside on WGD-derived syntenic blocks, suggesting dosage expansion in coca lineage. 3. Independent acquisition of coca PKSs contrasted with the Solanaceae PYKS lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior structural and biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. novogranatense genome PRJNA749480; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: copy-number or stacking with EnPKS1 may improve precursor supply for heterologous cocaine-pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes increased functional PKS dosage after WGD/WGT.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes MPOA to form the tropane ring-containing product ecgonone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms characteristic cocaine-branch tropane intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for tropane skeleton formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables ecgonone formation in cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP81AN15 uses a distinct active-site architecture involving T115, Q116, and D372 to position MPOA for oxidation to ecgonone. 2. Mutating these residues strongly reduced or abolished activity. 3. Comparative genomics showed CYP81AN15 likely arose by ancient tandem duplication and neofunctionalization, independently of the solanaceous CYP82M3 lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: AlphaFold2 structure prediction; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with LC-MS product detection; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous transient assay in N. benthamiana leaves with deuterated MPOA feeding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. novogranatense genome PRJNA749480; structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: essential skeleton-forming P450 for reconstructing the cocaine branch; active-site residues provide engineering handles for substrate specificity redesign
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Key catalytic residues coexist only in E. novogranatense and Rhizophoraceae homologs according to paper.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: tropinone/ecgonone-type tropane skeleton product in hyoscyamine route
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes MPOA to form the tropane skeleton in hyoscyamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms characteristic hyoscyamine-branch tropane intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for tropane skeleton formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables tropane skeleton biosynthesis in Solanaceae through a distinct P450 solution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82M3 uses a different active-site architecture from CYP81AN15, relying on R123 and D316 for MPOA positioning. 2. Mutating D316 abolished activity and mutating R123 drastically reduced activity. 3. Comparative genomics indicated CYP82M3 was independently recruited in Solanaceae, separate from CYP81AN15 evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: AlphaFold2 structure prediction; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis; in vitro microsomal enzyme assay with LC-MS; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro microsomal assay with MPOA and NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: A. acutangulus genome PRJNA752913; structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: essential oxidation catalyst for medicinal tropane alkaloid engineering in Solanaceae or heterologous chassis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional protein assayed was AbCYP82M3 from Atropa belladonna, while applied-species genomic inference includes A. acutangulus.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ecgonone derivative branch leading to cocaine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates the carboxyl group of ecgonone to produce methylecgonone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate precursor toward cocaine and ecgonone derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stabilizes the carboxyl group of ecgonone and directs metabolic flux to ecgonone derivatives including cocaine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnMT4 is an E. novogranatense-specific ecgonone methyltransferase that produces the characteristic 2-substituted carboxymethyl feature of cocaine. 2. Molecular docking and mutagenesis identified Q25, W157, E216, and S153 as critical, with E216 and S153 decisive for ecgonone specificity. 3. Comparative genomics and mutational conversion of EnMT1 support EnMT4 evolution from salicylic acid methyltransferases through lineage-specific duplication and key amino-acid substitutions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; phylogeny; AlphaFold2 docking; in vitro enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; gain-of-function mutation of EnMT1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro assay with ecgonone and SAM
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. novogranatense genome PRJNA749480; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: powerful branch-directing enzyme for engineering cocaine/ecgonone-derivative production; key residues E216 and S153 are useful for rational redesign of SABATH enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evolved after Erythroxylaceae-Rhizophoraceae divergence via likely LTR-RT-mediated duplication.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cocaine pathway evolution / precursor methyltransferase recruitment
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ancestral SABATH template for ecgonone methyltransferase evolution
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: salicylic acid; engineered enzyme also ecgonone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: methyl salicylate; engineered mutant also methylecgonone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: native enzyme methylates salicylic acid; mutational introduction of E216 and S153 confers ecgonone methylation activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: ancestral donor/template for evolution of branch-directing cocaine enzyme EnMT4
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: evolutionary precursor, not native cocaine step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: demonstrates how SAMT-like enzymes can evolve ecgonone methyltransferase activity relevant to cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnMT1 is a SAMT-like homolog syntenic with PtSAMT and methylates salicylic acid in vitro. 2. Introducing L216E grants ecgonone methylation activity, and combined L216E/Y153S increases this activity four-fold. 3. These experiments provide direct evidence that EnMT4 evolved from SAMT-like ancestors such as EnMT1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; site-directed mutagenesis; comparative genomics; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro assay with salicylic acid or ecgonone and SAM
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful scaffold for protein engineering of ecgonone methyltransferase activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally mutated in Results to prove EnMT4 origin.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cocaine pathway evolution / precursor methyltransferase recruitment
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ancestral SABATH template for ecgonone methyltransferase evolution
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates salicylic acid in vitro as a SAMT-like homolog of EnMT4
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: ancestral donor/template for evolution of ecgonone methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: evolutionary precursor, not native cocaine step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports inference that cocaine-specific EnMT4 evolved from salicylic acid methyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnMT3 was biochemically validated as a salicylic acid methyltransferase. 2. It clusters phylogenetically with EnMT1-4 and PtSAMT. 3. Together with synteny evidence, it supports EnMT4 derivation from SAMT ancestors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay with salicylic acid; comparative genomics; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro assay with salicylic acid and SAM
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential scaffold for evolving new alkaloid methyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as evolutionary evidence for EnMT4 origin.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: tropinone or related keto intermediate on tropane ring
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces the keto moiety on the tropane ring in hyoscyamine biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: postmodifies tropane skeleton toward hyoscyamine branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports reduction step specific to hyoscyamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI is the reductase used in the hyoscyamine pathway. 2. Synteny comparisons suggested TRI is likely a Solanaceae-specific duplication. 3. It exemplifies independent recruitment of postmodification reductases relative to the cocaine pathway MecgoR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior pathway characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: necessary tailoring enzyme for medicinal tropane pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as part of lineage-specific postmodification evolution.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: methylecgonone or related keto intermediate on tropane ring
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces the keto moiety on the tropane ring in the cocaine pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: postmodifies tropane skeleton toward cocaine branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports reduction step in cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MecgoR is the reductase used in cocaine biosynthesis, contrasting with TRI in hyoscyamine biosynthesis. 2. Comparative genomics placed MecgoR on a synteny block well conserved across eudicots. 3. Solanaceous MecgoR-like genes show little root expression, implying minimal contribution to hyoscyamine-associated tropinone reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; expression comparison; prior pathway characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics; transcript data from prior study context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important reductase for full reconstitution of the cocaine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes tomato SlMecgoR (SlAKR4B) has alternative native activities and low root expression.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: tropane alcohol intermediate and aromatic acyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes esterification of the tropane ring, shunting precursor skeleton into hyoscyamine/scopolamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly commits flux into hyoscyamine/scopolamine branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: channels tropane precursor toward hyoscyamine and scopolamine production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LS catalyzes the esterification reaction critical for shunting the tropane skeleton into hyoscyamine/scopolamine. 2. Syntenic orthologs of AaLS were found only in hyoscyamine-producing Datura stramonium among available solanaceous genomes. 3. This restricted distribution supports lineage-specific recruitment of acyltransferases in medicinal tropane pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior functional genomics characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: A. acutangulus genome PRJNA752913; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: critical branch enzyme for engineering hyoscyamine/scopolamine production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on its genomic distribution rather than new biochemical assays.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: cocaine precursor tropane alcohol intermediate and acyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: BAHD acyltransferase catalyzing the last step of cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CS is the BAHD acyltransferase catalyzing the last step in cocaine biosynthesis. 2. Comparative genomics found no close orthologs in examined relatives but identified many E. novogranatense-specific tandem BAHD genes adjacent to EnCS. 3. LTR-RT-mediated expansion likely generated the BAHD templates from which CS evolved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. novogranatense genome PRJNA749480; comparative genomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: essential terminal enzyme for reconstructing or engineering cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lineage-specific emergence linked to tandem BAHD expansion.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: cocaine precursor tropane alcohol intermediate and acyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: BAHD enzyme reportedly capable of catalyzing the same reaction as EnCS
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: alternative enzyme for terminal acylation step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: provides evidence of duplicate BAHD recruitment for cocaine-forming acylation chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EnBAHD8 is one of the tandem BAHD genes adjacent to EnCS. 2. Its product was reported to catalyze the same reaction as EnCS. 3. The study infers that EnBAHD8 and EnCS arose from LTR-RT-mediated BAHD gene expansion in Erythroxylaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; prior biochemical characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential alternative terminal acyltransferase for metabolic engineering of cocaine-like products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because paper explicitly highlights same catalytic function as EnCS.
